prior_findings.title: Reminder text contradiction — minimal/trivial listed as bypass phrases
prior_findings.evidence: README.md Phase 1 reminder text (lines 278-283) now lists only ['be quick', 'just do it', 'trivial fix'] and includes explicit parenthetical: 'Bare "minimal" / "trivial" are NOT accepted — they appear too often in technical prose.' The test_hook1_normal.py shape assertion will catch any regression against the old text.

prior_findings.title: Marker file path not anchored to repo root
prior_findings.evidence: README.md Phase 3 Steps (lines 376-384) now calls git rev-parse --show-toplevel FIRST (single call), then uses <repo_root>/specs/.tlmforge_active_feature for the marker read and <repo_root>/specs/<feature>/agent_verification/ for the glob. test_hook3_cwd_subdirectory.py description (lines 427-428) explicitly states 'BOTH marker file AND glob' resolve correctly.

new_findings.finding: Phase 3 specifies that corrupt final_audit JSON files are skipped (JSONDecodeError -> continue), but does not specify the behavior when a PSR marker file EXISTS with a missing verdict_sha field (not wrong, not corrupt, just absent). If the implementation treats absent-field as a match, a manually written PSR marker without verdict_sha silently bypasses the gate. Correct intent is: absent verdict_sha in PSR marker -> treat as no valid PSR -> block.
new_findings.suggested_fix: During Phase 3 TDD, add test_hook3_psr_marker_missing_verdict_sha.py: PSR marker file exists, verdict_sha field absent -> hook returns exit(2). Clarify in implementation comments that absent field is treated as mismatch, not match.
